title: GPIO Fundamentals
description: Core concepts of GPIO pins, digital signals, modes, and electrical characteristics of microcontroller I/O.

models.name: Arduino Uno
models.description: 3D Arduino Uno model used for interactive exploration.

sections.title: What is a GPIO Pin
sections.objective: Understand the role and purpose of GPIO pins

visual.title: GPIO connects software to the physical world

key_points: GPIO stands for General Purpose Input Output
GPIO pins are used to interface a microcontroller with external components
They can be configured as input or output
They are one of the main ways a microcontroller interacts with the environment

concepts.name: GPIO Pin
concepts.definition: A configurable digital pin on a microcontroller used for input or output operations

questions: What is the purpose of a GPIO pin?
Why are GPIO pins important in embedded systems?

sections.title: Digital Signals: HIGH and LOW
sections.objective: Understand how digital signals are represented electrically
key_points: Digital systems use two discrete states: HIGH and LOW
HIGH corresponds to a voltage level (e.g., 3.3V or 5V depending on the system)
LOW corresponds to 0V (ground)
These states represent binary values 1 and 0

concepts.name: Digital HIGH
concepts.definition: A voltage level interpreted as logical 1

concepts.name: Digital LOW
concepts.definition: A voltage level interpreted as logical 0

questions: What does a HIGH signal represent?
What is the voltage level of a LOW signal?

sections.title: Input vs Output Modes
sections.objective: Understand how GPIO pins are configured and used

visual.title: A pin can listen or speak

key_points: GPIO pins can operate in input or output mode
Input mode allows reading external signals
Output mode allows driving signals to external components
A pin must be explicitly configured in one mode at a time
Mode selection is done through firmware or registers

concepts.name: Input Mode
concepts.definition: Configuration where the GPIO pin reads external electrical signals

concepts.name: Output Mode
concepts.definition: Configuration where the GPIO pin generates electrical signals

questions: When would you configure a GPIO pin as input?
When would you configure a GPIO pin as output?

sections.title: Electrical Characteristics of GPIO Pins
sections.objective: Understand limitations and electrical behavior of GPIO pins

items.label: Risk
items.detail: too much current can damage hardware

key_points: GPIO pins operate at specific voltage levels defined by the microcontroller
Each pin has a maximum current it can safely source or sink
Exceeding current limits can damage the microcontroller
GPIO pins are not designed to drive high-power loads directly
Proper circuit design is required to protect the pin

concepts.name: Current Limit
concepts.definition: The maximum electrical current a GPIO pin can safely handle

concepts.name: Voltage Level
concepts.definition: The operating voltage used to represent HIGH and LOW states

questions: Why must we respect current limits on GPIO pins?
What could happen if a pin sources too much current?

sections.title: Applications of GPIO
sections.objective: Understand how GPIO concepts are applied in real-world systems

visual.title: GPIO is a bridge to real devices

key_points: GPIO pins are used to control actuators and read sensors
Examples include buttons, switches, LEDs, displays, and motors (via drivers)
GPIO output can be used to trigger external circuits
GPIO input can be used to detect user interaction or environmental signals
External components often require additional circuitry such as resistors or drivers

concepts.name: Actuator
concepts.definition: A device controlled by a microcontroller (e.g., LED, motor, buzzer)

concepts.name: Sensor
concepts.definition: A device that provides input signals to a microcontroller

questions: Give an example of a device controlled by a GPIO pin
Give an example of a device that sends signals to a GPIO pin

applications.name: LED Control
applications.description: An LED connected to GPIO number 4 should blink on and off every second. Write the code to achieve this behavior.

summary: GPIO pins are configurable interfaces for input and output
Digital signals are represented as HIGH and LOW voltage levels
GPIO pins operate in either input or output mode
Electrical constraints such as current limits must be respected
GPIO is used as a bridge between software and the physical world
